benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of the main idea and supporting details in conversations, presentations, and short discussions on familiar topics.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to identify the main idea and supporting details from audio or visual materials, such as conversations or presentations. Items can include multiple-choice questions that ask students to select the correct main idea or detail from a list of options.
essential_questions: What strategies can I use to identify the main idea in a conversation?
How do supporting details enhance the main idea?
In what ways can understanding the main idea improve my communication skills?
learning_targets: I can identify the main idea in conversations.
I can recognize supporting details that relate to the main idea.
I can summarize the main points of a discussion.
sample_assessment: What is the main idea of the conversation about weekend plans? A) Going to the beach B) Cooking dinner C) Watching a movie

benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of the main idea and supporting details on familiar and unfamiliar topics.
item_specs: Assessment items should challenge students to analyze both familiar and unfamiliar topics, requiring them to extract main ideas and supporting details from various texts or discussions. Items may include short answer questions where students explain their reasoning.
essential_questions: How can I differentiate between main ideas and details in unfamiliar topics?
What techniques can I use to understand complex ideas?
Why is it important to recognize main ideas in various contexts?
learning_targets: I can analyze main ideas in both familiar and unfamiliar topics.
I can explain how supporting details relate to the main idea.
I can apply my understanding of main ideas to new information.
sample_assessment: Which statement best represents the main idea of the article about renewable energy? A) Renewable energy is important B) Solar panels are expensive C) Wind energy is not effective

benchmark: Follow informal presentations on a variety of topics.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students' ability to follow and comprehend informal presentations. Multiple-choice or true/false questions can be used to assess understanding of key points presented in these informal settings.
essential_questions: What are the key elements of an informal presentation?
How can I effectively take notes during a presentation?
What role does audience engagement play in understanding presentations?
learning_targets: I can follow along with informal presentations.
I can identify key points in a presentation.
I can engage with the content of a presentation.
sample_assessment: During the informal presentation about hobbies, what was one key point mentioned? A) Hobbies are boring B) Hobbies can be relaxing C) Hobbies are only for kids

benchmark: Confirm understanding of the message and purpose of a variety of authentic sources found in the target culture such as TV, radio, podcasts, and videos.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to evaluate and confirm their understanding of messages from authentic sources such as TV shows, podcasts, or videos. Items may include analysis of clips or excerpts followed by questions that assess comprehension and interpretation.
essential_questions: How do authentic sources reflect cultural values?
What strategies help me understand messages in media?
Why is it important to analyze different types of media?
learning_targets: I can analyze messages from various authentic sources.
I can confirm my understanding of cultural contexts in media.
I can discuss the purpose of different media formats.
sample_assessment: What was the main purpose of the podcast episode about cultural festivals? A) To entertain B) To inform about traditions C) To criticize festivals

benchmark: Identify the main idea and supporting details from discussions and interviews on familiar topics.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students' ability to identify the main idea and supporting details from discussions and interviews. Multiple-choice questions can assess their comprehension of the content presented in these formats.
essential_questions: How can I extract main ideas from spoken discussions?
What techniques can help me remember details from interviews?
Why is it important to listen actively during discussions?
learning_targets: I can identify the main idea from discussions.
I can recognize supporting details in interviews.
I can summarize information from spoken content.
sample_assessment: In the interview about career choices, what was the main idea expressed? A) Career choices are easy B) Career choices require careful thought C) Career choices don't matter

benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of complex directions and instructions, in unfamiliar settings.
item_specs: Assessment items should present students with a variety of complex directions and instructions in unfamiliar contexts, requiring them to demonstrate comprehension through multiple-choice, true/false, or short answer formats. Items should assess the ability to interpret and follow directions accurately.
essential_questions: How do I approach understanding complex instructions?
What strategies can I use to clarify unfamiliar directions?
In what ways can context help me understand complex tasks?
learning_targets: I can follow complex directions in new situations.
I can ask questions to clarify instructions I don't understand.
I can summarize complex instructions in my own words.
sample_assessment: Which of the following best describes the first step in the instructions provided?

benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of the main idea and supporting details in texts on familiar and unfamiliar topics.
item_specs: Items should require students to identify the main idea and supporting details from texts, both familiar and unfamiliar. They should include multiple-choice questions that ask students to select the main idea from a list of options, as well as short answer questions that require explanation of supporting details.
essential_questions: How can I identify the main idea in a text?
What role do supporting details play in understanding a text?
How does context influence my understanding of a text's main idea?
learning_targets: I can identify the main idea in a text.
I can explain how supporting details relate to the main idea.
I can summarize texts by focusing on the main idea and key details.
sample_assessment: What is the main idea of the following passage?

benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of the main idea and supporting details in fictional literary texts containing unfamiliar vocabulary that can be interpreted in context.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students' ability to extract the main idea and supporting details from fictional literary texts that include unfamiliar vocabulary. Items may include multiple-choice questions that ask about the main idea and context clues for vocabulary, as well as short answer questions requiring textual evidence.
essential_questions: How do I determine the main idea in a fictional text?
What strategies can I use to understand unfamiliar vocabulary in stories?
How do supporting details enhance my understanding of a narrative?
learning_targets: I can identify the main idea in a fictional text.
I can use context clues to understand unfamiliar words.
I can discuss how supporting details contribute to the story.
sample_assessment: Which detail from the story best supports the main idea?

benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of general written information presented through a variety of sources and intended for practical applications in academic and workplace contexts.
item_specs: Items should assess students' ability to understand general written information from various sources, focusing on practical applications in academic and workplace contexts. Multiple-choice and short answer formats should be used to evaluate comprehension and application of the information presented.
essential_questions: How can I apply written information to real-world situations?
What types of sources provide the most useful information for academic purposes?
How do I evaluate the credibility of information from different sources?
learning_targets: I can understand and apply general information from texts.
I can identify reliable sources of information.
I can summarize information for practical use.
sample_assessment: Which of the following sources would provide the most reliable information for your project?

benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of the main idea and supporting details when gathering information from texts that contain unfamiliar vocabulary, when reading for information.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to gather information from texts that contain unfamiliar vocabulary, focusing on identifying the main idea and supporting details. Items should include multiple-choice questions and short answer responses that ask students to explain how they derived meaning from the text.
essential_questions: How can I effectively gather information from complex texts?
What strategies help me understand unfamiliar vocabulary while reading?
How do I determine the main idea when reading for information?
learning_targets: I can gather information from texts with unfamiliar vocabulary.
I can identify the main idea and supporting details in informational texts.
I can use context to help understand new words while reading.
sample_assessment: What is the main idea of the following informational text?

benchmark: State and support different points of views and take an active part in discussions.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to analyze different viewpoints on a given topic and articulate their own perspective, supported by evidence or reasoning. Items may include scenarios where students must choose the best response or argument based on the viewpoints presented.
essential_questions: How can understanding different perspectives enhance a discussion?
What strategies can be used to respectfully disagree with someone?
Why is it important to support your point of view with evidence?
learning_targets: I can identify different points of view on a topic.
I can support my opinions with reasons and examples.
I can engage in discussions by listening and responding to others.
sample_assessment: Which of the following statements best supports the viewpoint that technology improves education?

benchmark: Sustain a conversation in uncomplicated situations on a variety of topics.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students' ability to sustain a conversation by asking and answering questions on various topics. Items may include role-play scenarios or multiple-choice questions that assess comprehension and engagement in dialogue.
essential_questions: What makes a conversation engaging?
How can I keep a conversation going?
What topics are easiest to discuss with others?
learning_targets: I can ask questions to keep a conversation going.
I can share my thoughts on familiar topics.
I can respond to others in a conversation.
sample_assessment: What is the best way to respond if someone asks you about your favorite hobby?

benchmark: Express degrees of emotion and respond appropriately to the feelings and emotions of others.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to identify and express emotions in various scenarios, as well as respond appropriately to the emotions of others. Items may include situational prompts or role-play assessments that gauge emotional intelligence and empathetic responses.
essential_questions: How can expressing emotions improve communication?
Why is it important to recognize the feelings of others?
What are effective ways to respond to someone who is upset?
learning_targets: I can express my feelings clearly.
I can recognize emotions in others.
I can respond appropriately to someone else's feelings.
sample_assessment: If a friend tells you they are sad, what is the best way to respond?

benchmark: Exchange detailed information related to areas of mutual interest including careers of choice, job opportunities, etc.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students' ability to exchange detailed information about mutual interests, particularly related to careers and job opportunities. Items may include scenarios where students must provide or request specific information and assess their ability to communicate effectively.
essential_questions: What information is important to share when discussing careers?
How can I learn more about someone else's job interests?
Why is networking important in career development?
learning_targets: I can share detailed information about my career interests.
I can ask questions about someone else's job or career.
I can discuss job opportunities in a clear manner.
sample_assessment: What is the best question to ask someone about their job?

benchmark: Initiate, maintain, and end a conversation on a variety of familiar topics.
item_specs: Assessment items should evaluate students' ability to initiate, maintain, and conclude conversations on familiar topics. Items may include prompts for role-playing conversations or multiple-choice questions that assess understanding of conversational flow.
essential_questions: What are effective ways to start a conversation?
How can I smoothly end a conversation?
What familiar topics can I discuss with others?
learning_targets: I can start a conversation about a familiar topic.
I can keep a conversation going by asking questions.
I can end a conversation politely.
sample_assessment: What is a good way to start a conversation with someone you just met?

benchmark: Often use circumlocution when faced with unfamiliar vocabulary and difficult language structures.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to demonstrate their ability to use circumlocution effectively when faced with unfamiliar vocabulary. Items may include scenarios where students must describe a word or concept without using the exact term, showcasing their understanding and language skills.
essential_questions: How can I describe a word or concept without using the exact term?
What strategies can I use to communicate when I don't know a specific word?
Why is circumlocution important in language learning?
learning_targets: I can use different words to explain unfamiliar vocabulary.
I can communicate effectively even when I don't know the exact term.
I can ask for clarification when I encounter difficult language.
sample_assessment: Which of the following is an example of circumlocution? A) Using a synonym B) Describing a 'dog' as 'a four-legged pet that barks' C) Saying 'I don't know' D) Using the word 'canine'.

benchmark: Ask for, follow, and give directions in complex situations.
item_specs: Assessment items should involve real-life scenarios where students must ask for, follow, and give directions. Items may include role-playing exercises or written prompts that require students to navigate complex situations, demonstrating their ability to communicate effectively.
essential_questions: How do I ask for directions in a way that is clear and understandable?
What details are important when giving directions?
How can I ensure I understand directions given to me?
learning_targets: I can ask for directions in a clear manner.
I can follow complex directions accurately.
I can give detailed directions to someone else.
sample_assessment: If you are lost in a city, which of the following questions would best help you find your way? A) Where is the nearest store? B) Can you tell me how to get to the library from here? C) What time is it? D) Do you like this city?

benchmark: Describe and elaborate on a personal situation or problem using details.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to describe and elaborate on a personal situation or problem. Items may include prompts that ask students to provide detailed accounts, focusing on their ability to express thoughts clearly and use descriptive language.
essential_questions: How can I effectively share my personal experiences?
What details make a story more engaging?
Why is it important to elaborate on my thoughts and feelings?
learning_targets: I can describe a personal situation with clarity.
I can elaborate on my thoughts and feelings.
I can use descriptive language to engage my audience.
sample_assessment: Which of the following sentences best elaborates on a personal experience? A) I went to the beach. B) I went to the beach and felt the warm sand between my toes while listening to the waves crash. C) The beach is nice. D) I like the ocean.

benchmark: Present information on familiar topics with clarity and detail using multimedia resources.
item_specs: Assessment items should evaluate students' ability to present information using multimedia resources. Items may include projects where students create presentations that require clear organization, effective use of visuals, and detailed explanations of familiar topics.
essential_questions: What makes a presentation effective?
How can multimedia enhance my message?
In what ways can I organize my information for clarity?
learning_targets: I can present information clearly using multimedia.
I can organize my presentation to engage my audience.
I can use visuals to support my spoken content.
sample_assessment: What is the most effective way to enhance your presentation? A) Use lots of text B) Include relevant images and videos C) Speak without preparation D) Avoid using any visuals.

benchmark: Present viewpoints on an issue and support opinions with clarity and detail.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to present their viewpoints on an issue, supporting their opinions with clear reasoning and evidence. Items may include debates, written essays, or presentations that challenge students to articulate their thoughts effectively.
essential_questions: How can I support my opinion with evidence?
What strategies can I use to present my viewpoint clearly?
Why is it important to consider multiple perspectives on an issue?
learning_targets: I can present my viewpoint on an issue clearly.
I can support my opinions with logical reasoning.
I can consider and address counterarguments in my presentation.
sample_assessment: Which of the following statements best supports an opinion? A) I think recycling is important. B) Recycling helps reduce waste and conserve resources, which is essential for our environment. C) Many people recycle. D) Recycling is a good idea.

benchmark: Describe personal experiences and interests with clarity and detail.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to articulate their personal experiences and interests with specific details. Items may include prompts for written responses or oral presentations that assess clarity, organization, and detail in communication.
essential_questions: How can I effectively share my personal experiences with others?
What details make a story more engaging and clear?
Why is it important to express my interests clearly?
learning_targets: I can describe my personal experiences in detail.
I can share my interests with clarity.
I can organize my thoughts to communicate effectively.
sample_assessment: Which of the following sentences best describes a personal experience with clarity and detail? A) I went to the beach. B) Last summer, I visited the beach with my family, where we built sandcastles and swam in the ocean.

benchmark: Produce reports and multimedia compositions in order to present a group project.
item_specs: Assessment items should evaluate students' ability to collaborate on group projects and produce reports or multimedia compositions. Items may include rubrics for assessing the effectiveness of the presentation and the quality of the multimedia elements used.
essential_questions: How can multimedia enhance my group project?
What roles can team members take in a group project?
How do I effectively communicate our findings to an audience?
learning_targets: I can work collaboratively on a group project.
I can create multimedia presentations that support our project.
I can present our group's findings clearly.
sample_assessment: What is the most effective way to present a group project using multimedia? A) Use only text slides. B) Incorporate images, videos, and graphs to support our findings.

benchmark: Use paraphrasing, circumlocution, and illustrations to make self more clearly understood when relating experiences and retelling a story.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students' ability to use paraphrasing and circumlocution to clarify their storytelling. Items may include scenarios where students must retell a story using different words or illustrations to enhance understanding.
essential_questions: How can I make my story more understandable?
What techniques can I use to clarify my message?
Why is it important to paraphrase when telling a story?
learning_targets: I can use paraphrasing to clarify my stories.
I can illustrate my experiences to enhance understanding.
I can retell a story using different words.
sample_assessment: Which of the following is an example of effective paraphrasing? A) I went to the store. B) I visited the shop to buy some groceries.

benchmark: Formulate and deliver a presentation on an assigned topic using multimedia resources to support the presentation.
item_specs: Assessment items should assess students' ability to formulate and deliver presentations on assigned topics using multimedia resources. Items may include rubrics that evaluate content organization, use of multimedia, and delivery skills.
essential_questions: What makes a presentation engaging?
How can I effectively use multimedia in my presentation?
What strategies can I use to organize my presentation?
learning_targets: I can create a well-organized presentation on a given topic.
I can use multimedia resources to support my presentation.
I can deliver my presentation confidently.
sample_assessment: What is the best way to organize a presentation on a specific topic? A) Start with the conclusion. B) Begin with an introduction, followed by main points, and end with a conclusion.

benchmark: Write communications, narratives, descriptions, and explanations on familiar topics using connected, detailed paragraphs.
item_specs: Assessment items should evaluate students' ability to write connected, detailed paragraphs on familiar topics. Items may include prompts that require students to write narratives, descriptions, or explanations, focusing on coherence and detail.
essential_questions: How can I connect my ideas in writing?
What details make my writing more vivid?
Why is it important to write in complete paragraphs?
learning_targets: I can write connected paragraphs on familiar topics.
I can include details in my writing to enhance clarity.
I can create narratives and descriptions that engage the reader.
sample_assessment: Which of the following sentences is part of a well-connected paragraph? A) I like pizza. B) Pizza is my favorite food because it is delicious, and I enjoy trying different toppings.

benchmark: Describe, in writing, personal experiences and interests with clarity and detail.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to write a short narrative about a personal experience, focusing on clarity and detail. Items may include prompts that guide students to elaborate on their feelings, thoughts, and sensory details related to their experiences.
essential_questions: How can I effectively convey my personal experiences to others?
What details make a personal story engaging and relatable?
Why is clarity important when describing personal experiences?
learning_targets: I can describe my personal experiences in writing.
I can use sensory details to enhance my writing.
I can organize my thoughts clearly in a written format.
sample_assessment: Which of the following sentences best describes a personal experience with clarity and detail?

benchmark: Present, in writing, viewpoints on an issue and support opinion with clarity and detail.
item_specs: Assessment items should ask students to write an opinion piece on a given issue, requiring them to present their viewpoint clearly and support it with detailed arguments. Items may include prompts that encourage students to consider counterarguments and provide evidence.
essential_questions: How can I effectively support my opinion in writing?
What makes an argument persuasive?
Why is it important to consider multiple viewpoints on an issue?
learning_targets: I can present my viewpoint on an issue in writing.
I can support my opinion with clear arguments and evidence.
I can address counterarguments in my writing.
sample_assessment: What is the main purpose of an opinion piece?

benchmark: Provide clear and detailed information in writing on academic and work topics with clarity and detail.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to write informative texts on academic or work-related topics, ensuring they provide clear and detailed information. Items may include prompts that guide students to define terms, explain concepts, and present information logically.
essential_questions: How can I provide clear information about academic topics?
What details are essential when writing about work-related subjects?
Why is clarity important in academic writing?
learning_targets: I can write clearly about academic topics.
I can provide detailed information in my writing.
I can organize my writing logically.
sample_assessment: Which of the following is a key component of writing about an academic topic?

benchmark: Describe, in writing, events in chronological order.
item_specs: Assessment items should guide students to write narratives that describe events in chronological order. Items may include prompts that encourage students to use transition words and phrases to indicate the sequence of events.
essential_questions: How can I effectively organize events in chronological order?
What transition words help clarify the sequence of events?
Why is chronological order important in storytelling?
learning_targets: I can describe events in chronological order in my writing.
I can use transition words to indicate sequence.
I can organize my narrative logically.
sample_assessment: What is the purpose of using transition words in a narrative?

benchmark: Write about a story and describe reactions with clarity and detail.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to write a story that includes their reactions to events, focusing on clarity and detail. Items may include prompts that encourage students to reflect on their emotional responses and describe them vividly.
essential_questions: How can I convey my reactions to events in a story?
What details make my emotional responses clear to the reader?
Why is it important to include personal reactions in storytelling?
learning_targets: I can write a story that includes my reactions.
I can describe my emotions clearly in writing.
I can enhance my storytelling with detailed descriptions.
sample_assessment: Which of the following sentences best conveys a character's emotional reaction?

benchmark: Write a short essay or biography using descriptive details and a variety of sentence structure.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to demonstrate their ability to write a cohesive short essay or biography. Items may include prompts that ask for specific descriptive details and varied sentence structures, assessing both content and language mechanics.
essential_questions: How can descriptive details enhance a narrative?
What role does sentence variety play in effective writing?
learning_targets: I can write a short essay with descriptive details.
I can use a variety of sentence structures in my writing.
sample_assessment: Which of the following sentences uses a variety of sentence structures effectively?

benchmark: Investigate practices and perspectives of past and contemporary life in the target culture through a variety of media.
item_specs: Assessment items should engage students in analyzing various media sources to investigate cultural practices and perspectives. Items may include multimedia presentations, articles, or interviews, requiring students to synthesize information and draw conclusions about the target culture.
essential_questions: How do different media reflect the practices of a culture?
What can we learn about a culture by examining its contemporary life?
learning_targets: I can analyze media to understand cultural practices.
I can compare past and contemporary perspectives in the target culture.
sample_assessment: Which media source best illustrates the contemporary practices of the target culture?

benchmark: Apply language and behaviors that are appropriate to the target culture in an authentic situation.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students' ability to apply appropriate language and behaviors in simulated authentic situations. Items may include role-play scenarios or dialogues that require students to demonstrate cultural competence and language use.
essential_questions: What behaviors are considered appropriate in the target culture?
How can language use change depending on the context?
learning_targets: I can use appropriate language in a cultural context.
I can demonstrate behaviors that are acceptable in the target culture.
sample_assessment: In a role-play scenario, which response best demonstrates appropriate behavior in the target culture?

benchmark: Discuss historical events and their impact on current practices and perspectives in the target culture.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to discuss and analyze historical events and their impacts on current cultural practices and perspectives. Items may include essays, presentations, or discussions that encourage critical thinking and connections between history and contemporary culture.
essential_questions: How do historical events shape current cultural practices?
What perspectives can we gain from studying the past?
learning_targets: I can discuss the impact of historical events on culture.
I can analyze how past perspectives influence current practices.
sample_assessment: Which historical event had the most significant impact on the current practices of the target culture?

benchmark: Describe products of the target culture and their significance.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students' ability to describe cultural products and explain their significance. Items may include written descriptions, presentations, or visual projects that require students to articulate the importance of various cultural artifacts or practices.
essential_questions: What are some key products of the target culture?
Why is it important to understand the significance of cultural products?
learning_targets: I can describe products of the target culture.
I can explain the significance of cultural artifacts.
sample_assessment: Which of the following best describes a significant product of the target culture?

benchmark: Gather and interpret information from the target language to reinforce and further knowledge of other disciplines.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to gather information from various sources in the target language and interpret that information in the context of other academic disciplines. Items may include short answer responses or multiple-choice questions that assess comprehension and application of the gathered information.
essential_questions: How can information from the target language enhance my understanding of other subjects?
What strategies can I use to effectively gather information in the target language?
learning_targets: I can gather information from the target language.
I can interpret information to support my learning in other subjects.
sample_assessment: What is one way information from the target language can help you in your science class?

benchmark: Evaluate information from a variety of sources in the target language to present different viewpoints on community issues.
item_specs: Assessment items should prompt students to evaluate information from diverse sources in the target language, focusing on community issues. Items may include analytical essays or presentations that require students to synthesize viewpoints and present their findings.
essential_questions: How can evaluating different viewpoints help us understand community issues?
What sources can provide diverse perspectives on a community issue?
learning_targets: I can evaluate information from various sources in the target language.
I can present different viewpoints on community issues.
sample_assessment: Which of the following sources would provide the most diverse viewpoints on a local community issue?

benchmark: Compare own language and culture to the target language and culture, drawing conclusions about similarities and differences.
item_specs: Assessment items should encourage students to compare their own language and culture with the target language and culture. Items may include comparative essays or graphic organizers that highlight similarities and differences.
essential_questions: What are the key similarities and differences between my culture and the target culture?
How does language reflect cultural values?
learning_targets: I can compare my language to the target language.
I can identify similarities and differences between cultures.
sample_assessment: Which of the following statements best describes a similarity between your culture and the target culture?

benchmark: Analyze idiomatic expressions, proverbs, and their cultural significance in the target language.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to analyze idiomatic expressions and proverbs in the target language, focusing on their cultural significance. Items may include analysis essays or matching exercises that connect expressions to cultural contexts.
essential_questions: What do idiomatic expressions reveal about a culture?
How can understanding proverbs enhance my language skills?
learning_targets: I can analyze idiomatic expressions in the target language.
I can explain the cultural significance of proverbs.
sample_assessment: What does the idiom 'to kick the bucket' imply about cultural attitudes towards death?

benchmark: Analyze cultural perspectives and traditions of the target culture in comparison to own.
item_specs: Assessment items should challenge students to analyze and compare cultural perspectives and traditions of the target culture with their own. Items may include research projects or presentations that require deep analysis and critical thinking.
essential_questions: How do cultural traditions shape our identities?
In what ways can analyzing cultural perspectives foster understanding?
learning_targets: I can analyze cultural traditions of the target culture.
I can compare and contrast these traditions with my own.
sample_assessment: Which of the following best describes a tradition in the target culture that differs from your own?

benchmark: Use the target language in a variety of real-world settings for personal and community purposes.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to apply their language skills in real-world scenarios, demonstrating their ability to communicate effectively in various personal and community contexts. Items may include role-playing situations, dialogues, or reflective writing prompts.
essential_questions: How can I use the target language to connect with my community?
In what ways does language facilitate personal interactions in diverse settings?
What are some real-world scenarios where I can apply my language skills?
learning_targets: I can engage in conversations using the target language in community settings.
I can express my thoughts and ideas in the target language during personal interactions.
I can navigate real-world situations using the target language.
sample_assessment: Which of the following scenarios best demonstrates the use of the target language in a community setting?

benchmark: Access and evaluate information available through the target language and its cultures for personal growth.
item_specs: Assessment items should challenge students to critically analyze and synthesize information obtained from various sources in the target language. Items may include research projects, presentations, or reflective essays that require students to evaluate cultural contexts and their relevance to personal development.
essential_questions: How can I use information from other cultures to enhance my personal growth?
What strategies can I use to evaluate the credibility of information in the target language?
In what ways does understanding another culture contribute to my own identity?
learning_targets: I can access and interpret information from the target language.
I can evaluate cultural resources for personal development.
I can reflect on how cultural insights influence my perspective.
sample_assessment: Which of the following sources provides the most credible information about the culture of a Spanish-speaking country?

benchmark: Use context cues to identify the main idea and essential details on familiar topics expressed in short conversations, presentations, and messages.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students' ability to use context clues to determine the main idea and supporting details in short conversations or presentations. Items may include listening exercises, multiple-choice questions, or fill-in-the-blank activities that emphasize comprehension skills.
essential_questions: How can context clues help me understand spoken language?
What strategies can I use to identify the main idea in a conversation?
How do supporting details enhance my understanding of a message?
learning_targets: I can identify the main idea in short conversations.
I can use context clues to understand essential details.
I can summarize key points from oral presentations.
sample_assessment: What is the main idea of the following conversation about weekend plans?

benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of the main idea and essential details of short conversations and oral presentations.
item_specs: Assessment items should assess students' ability to demonstrate understanding of the main idea and essential details from short conversations and oral presentations. Items may include listening tasks, multiple-choice questions, or short answer responses that require students to articulate their understanding.
essential_questions: What techniques can I use to improve my listening comprehension?
How can I differentiate between main ideas and supporting details in spoken language?
Why is it important to understand the main idea in a conversation?
learning_targets: I can demonstrate understanding of the main idea in oral presentations.
I can identify essential details from spoken conversations.
I can respond appropriately to questions about what I heard.
sample_assessment: What essential detail can be inferred from the following oral presentation about healthy eating?

benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of the main idea and essential details in messages and announcements on familiar topics.
item_specs: Assessment items should evaluate students' ability to understand the main idea and essential details in messages and announcements on familiar topics. Items may include listening exercises, true/false questions, or multiple-choice items that test comprehension skills.
essential_questions: How can I effectively listen to announcements in the target language?
What strategies help me grasp the main idea of a spoken message?
Why is understanding announcements important in daily life?
learning_targets: I can understand the main idea in messages I hear.
I can identify key details in announcements.
I can summarize information from spoken messages.
sample_assessment: What is the main idea of the following announcement regarding school events?

benchmark: Identify key points and essential details on familiar topics presented in a variety of media.
item_specs: Assessment items should present a variety of media formats, such as videos, articles, or infographics. Students will be asked to identify key points and essential details from these media, requiring them to analyze and synthesize information effectively.
essential_questions: What are the key points presented in this media?
How do essential details support the main idea?
In what ways can different media formats convey similar information?
learning_targets: I can identify key points in a video.
I can summarize essential details from an article.
I can compare information from different media.
sample_assessment: What is the main idea presented in the video about recycling? A) It is expensive B) It helps the environment C) It is not necessary D) It is difficult

benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of the main idea and essential details from oral narration and stories on familiar topics.
item_specs: Items should focus on oral narratives, where students listen to stories and then answer questions about the main idea and essential details. The assessment should encourage students to recall specific information and make connections to familiar topics.
essential_questions: What is the main idea of the story?
What details support the main idea?
How does the story relate to my own experiences?
learning_targets: I can identify the main idea of a story I hear.
I can recall essential details from an oral narration.
I can relate the story to my own life.
sample_assessment: What is the main idea of the story about the lost puppy? A) Puppies are cute B) The puppy finds its way home C) Dogs are better than cats D) The puppy is hungry

benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of multiple-step directions and instructions, in familiar settings.
item_specs: Assessment items should present students with multiple-step directions in familiar contexts, such as classroom activities or daily routines. Students will demonstrate their understanding by following these directions accurately and reflecting on the process.
essential_questions: What steps do I need to follow to complete the task?
How can I ensure I understand the instructions?
What challenges might I face when following multiple-step directions?
learning_targets: I can follow multiple-step directions in class.
I can ask for clarification if I don't understand instructions.
I can complete a task by following given steps.
sample_assessment: What is the first step in the instructions to make a sandwich? A) Put the sandwich in the fridge B) Gather the ingredients C) Eat the sandwich D) Cut the sandwich in half

benchmark: Use context clues and background knowledge to demonstrate understanding of the main idea and essential details in texts that contain familiar themes.
item_specs: Items should require students to read texts that contain familiar themes and use context clues to determine the main idea and essential details. Students will analyze the text and demonstrate comprehension through targeted questions.
essential_questions: How do context clues help me understand the text?
What is the main idea based on the details provided?
How can I use my background knowledge to enhance my understanding?
learning_targets: I can use context clues to find the main idea.
I can identify essential details in a text.
I can relate background knowledge to what I read.
sample_assessment: What is the main idea of the passage about summer activities? A) Summer is hot B) People enjoy swimming and picnics C) Summer lasts three months D) Everyone goes on vacation

benchmark: Interpret written literary text in which the writer tells or asks about familiar topics.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on interpreting written literary texts that discuss familiar topics. Students will analyze the text to extract meaning and respond to questions that require deeper understanding and personal interpretation.
essential_questions: What message is the author trying to convey?
How do the characters or events relate to familiar topics?
What personal connections can I make with the text?
learning_targets: I can interpret the meaning of a literary text.
I can identify themes related to familiar topics.
I can express my personal connections to the text.
sample_assessment: What is the main message of the poem about friendship? A) Friends are important B) Being alone is better C) Friends can be annoying D) Friendship is overrated

benchmark: Determine the meaning of a message and identify the author's purpose of authentic written texts such as advertisements and public announcements.
item_specs: Assessment items should present authentic written texts, such as advertisements or public announcements, followed by questions that require students to identify the author's purpose and interpret the meaning of the message. Items may include multiple-choice questions, short answer responses, or matching exercises.
essential_questions: What techniques do authors use to convey their message?
How can understanding the author's purpose enhance comprehension of a text?
learning_targets: I can identify the author's purpose in a text.
I can explain the meaning of a message in advertisements.
sample_assessment: What is the primary purpose of the advertisement? A) To inform B) To entertain C) To persuade D) To describe

benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of vocabulary used in context when following written directions.
item_specs: Assessment items should present written directions that require students to demonstrate their understanding of vocabulary in context. Questions may include multiple-choice items that ask students to choose the correct meaning of a word based on its use in the directions or to complete sentences using appropriate vocabulary.
essential_questions: How does context help us understand unfamiliar words?
What strategies can we use to follow written directions accurately?
learning_targets: I can understand vocabulary used in context.
I can follow written directions accurately.
sample_assessment: In the following directions, what does the word 'assemble' mean? A) To take apart B) To put together C) To decorate D) To discard

benchmark: Initiate and engage in a conversation on familiar topics.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to initiate and engage in conversations about familiar topics. This could include role-play scenarios, where students must respond to prompts or questions, demonstrating their ability to maintain a conversation and use appropriate language and expressions.
essential_questions: What strategies can help us start a conversation?
How can we keep a conversation going?
learning_targets: I can start a conversation on a familiar topic.
I can engage in discussions with others.
sample_assessment: Which of the following is a good way to start a conversation? A) 'What time is it?' B) 'Hi, how was your weekend?' C) 'Can you help me?' D) 'I don't know.'

benchmark: Interact with others in everyday situations.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students' ability to interact in everyday situations. This may include scenarios where students must choose appropriate responses or actions based on given social contexts, using multiple-choice questions or role-play exercises.
essential_questions: How do we communicate effectively in everyday situations?
What are some common social interactions we encounter?
learning_targets: I can interact appropriately with others in everyday situations.
I can respond to social cues in conversations.
sample_assessment: In a store, if someone asks you for help, what is the best response? A) 'Leave me alone.' B) 'Sure, how can I help you?' C) 'I don't care.' D) 'Why are you asking me?'

benchmark: Express and react to feelings and emotions in real life situations.
item_specs: Assessment items should assess students' ability to express and react to feelings and emotions in real-life situations. This could involve scenarios where students must choose appropriate emotional responses or describe their feelings in response to prompts, using multiple-choice or short answer formats.
essential_questions: How can we express our feelings appropriately?
Why is it important to understand others' emotions?
learning_targets: I can express my feelings in different situations.
I can recognize and respond to others' emotions.
sample_assessment: If a friend is sad, what is the best way to respond? A) 'Stop being sad.' B) 'I understand how you feel.' C) 'It doesn't matter.' D) 'Just cheer up.'

benchmark: Exchange information about familiar academic and social topics including participation in an interview.
item_specs: Assessment items should include scenarios where students must demonstrate their ability to exchange information in an interview setting. Items may require students to select appropriate responses or fill in dialogue prompts based on given contexts.
essential_questions: How can effective communication enhance an interview experience?
What strategies can be used to ask and answer questions during an interview?
learning_targets: I can participate in an interview by asking and answering questions.
I can share information about familiar topics during a conversation.
sample_assessment: What is an appropriate question to ask during an interview about someone's favorite book?

benchmark: Initiate a conversation to meet basic needs in everyday situations both in and outside the classroom.
item_specs: Assessment items should present everyday scenarios where students must initiate conversations to meet basic needs. Items may involve selecting the best opening lines or responding to prompts in a dialogue format.
essential_questions: How can initiating a conversation help in everyday situations?
What are some effective ways to communicate basic needs?
learning_targets: I can start a conversation to ask for help or information.
I can communicate my needs in various everyday situations.
sample_assessment: Which phrase would you use to start a conversation when you need help finding a book?

benchmark: Recount and restate information received in a conversation in order to clarify meaning.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students' abilities to listen and restate information accurately. Items may include audio clips or written dialogues where students must identify key points and clarify meanings.
essential_questions: Why is it important to clarify information in a conversation?
How can restating information help in understanding?
learning_targets: I can listen to a conversation and restate important information.
I can ask questions to clarify what someone has said.
sample_assessment: After listening to a conversation about weekend plans, what is the best way to restate what you heard?

benchmark: Exchange general information about a few topics outside personal and academic fields of interest.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to exchange general information about various topics outside of their personal interests. Items may involve multiple-choice questions or dialogue completion tasks.
essential_questions: How can sharing general information enhance social interactions?
What topics are appropriate for casual conversations?
learning_targets: I can share general information about a variety of topics.
I can engage in conversations that go beyond my personal interests.
sample_assessment: Which topic would be suitable for a casual conversation with someone you just met?

benchmark: Initiate, engage, and exchange basic information to solve a problem.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students' abilities to initiate and engage in conversations aimed at solving problems. Items may include scenario-based questions where students must choose the best dialogue options to address a specific issue.
essential_questions: How can effective communication help solve problems?
What strategies can be used to exchange information when facing a challenge?
learning_targets: I can initiate a conversation to address a problem.
I can work with others to exchange information and find solutions.
sample_assessment: What is the first thing you should say to someone when you need help solving a problem?

benchmark: Present information on familiar topics using a series of sentences with sufficient details.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to present information on familiar topics using a series of sentences. Items may include prompts that ask students to elaborate on a topic, ensuring they provide sufficient details to convey their message effectively.
essential_questions: How can I effectively present information about a topic I know?
What details are necessary to make my presentation clear and engaging?
How does the structure of my sentences impact the clarity of my message?
learning_targets: I can present information clearly using complete sentences.
I can include relevant details in my presentations.
I can organize my thoughts logically when speaking.
sample_assessment: Which of the following is the best way to present information about your favorite animal?

benchmark: Describe people, objects, and situations using a series of sequenced sentences.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students' ability to describe people, objects, and situations using a series of sequenced sentences. Items may ask students to write or verbally describe a scenario or character, ensuring they maintain a logical flow.
essential_questions: What details help create a vivid description?
How can I organize my sentences to describe something effectively?
Why is sequencing important in descriptions?
learning_targets: I can describe people and objects using detailed sentences.
I can sequence my sentences to improve clarity.
I can use descriptive language to enhance my descriptions.
sample_assessment: Which sentence best describes a sunset? A) The sun goes down. B) The sun sets, painting the sky with vibrant oranges and pinks.

benchmark: Express needs, wants, and plans using a series of sentences that include essential details.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to express their needs, wants, and plans using a series of sentences that include essential details. Items may involve scenarios where students must articulate their desires or intentions clearly.
essential_questions: How can I clearly express what I need or want?
What details are important when sharing my plans?
How do my sentences help convey my intentions?
learning_targets: I can express my needs and wants clearly.
I can share my plans using detailed sentences.
I can articulate my thoughts in a logical manner.
sample_assessment: Which of the following sentences best expresses a need? A) I need to go. B) I need to go to the store to buy milk for breakfast.

benchmark: Provide a logical sequence of instructions on how to make something or complete a task.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students' ability to provide a logical sequence of instructions on how to make something or complete a task. Items may require students to write or verbally present step-by-step instructions, ensuring clarity and completeness.
essential_questions: What steps are necessary to complete a task successfully?
How can I ensure my instructions are easy to follow?
Why is the order of instructions important?
learning_targets: I can provide clear instructions for completing a task.
I can organize my instructions in a logical sequence.
I can use precise language to enhance understanding.
sample_assessment: What is the first step in making a sandwich? A) Eat the sandwich. B) Gather all the ingredients.

benchmark: Present a short skit or play using well-structured sentences.
item_specs: Assessment items should assess students' ability to present a short skit or play using well-structured sentences. Items may involve prompts for students to create dialogues or scenes that demonstrate their understanding of character and plot development.
essential_questions: How can I structure my sentences for a skit?
What elements make a performance engaging?
How do I convey character through dialogue?
learning_targets: I can create a short skit using well-structured sentences.
I can engage an audience through my performance.
I can develop characters and dialogue effectively.
sample_assessment: Which of the following lines is best for a character in a skit? A) I am happy. B) I can't believe we finally won the game! This is the best day ever!

benchmark: Describe events in chronological order using connected sentences with relevant details.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to arrange events in chronological order and provide connected sentences that include relevant details. Items may include prompts that ask students to describe a sequence of events, ensuring clarity and coherence in their writing.
essential_questions: How can I effectively organize events in a sequence?
What details are important to include when describing an event?
How does the order of events affect the understanding of a story?
learning_targets: I can describe events in the order they happened.
I can use connected sentences to explain my thoughts.
I can include relevant details in my descriptions.
sample_assessment: Which of the following sentences best describes the events of a birthday party in chronological order?

benchmark: Write on familiar topics and experiences using main ideas and supporting details.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students' ability to identify and write main ideas and supporting details on familiar topics. Items may include prompts that ask students to write a short paragraph or essay that clearly states a main idea and provides relevant details to support it.
essential_questions: What is the main idea of my writing?
How can I support my main idea with details?
Why is it important to have a clear main idea in my writing?
learning_targets: I can write about familiar topics using a main idea.
I can include supporting details in my writing.
I can organize my thoughts clearly.
sample_assessment: What is the main idea of the following paragraph about summer vacation?

benchmark: Describe a familiar event or situation using a variety of sentences and with supporting details.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to describe a familiar event or situation using a variety of sentence structures and supporting details. Items may include prompts that encourage creativity and detail in descriptions, assessing the use of different sentence types.
essential_questions: How can I use different types of sentences to enhance my writing?
What details make my description more vivid?
Why is it important to vary my sentence structure?
learning_targets: I can describe an event using different types of sentences.
I can add supporting details to make my writing more interesting.
I can express my thoughts clearly in writing.
sample_assessment: Which sentence best describes a family picnic using a variety of sentence structures?

benchmark: Express and support opinions on familiar topics using a series of sentences.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students expressing and supporting their opinions on familiar topics through a series of sentences. Items may include prompts that ask students to write a short essay where they state their opinion and provide reasons and examples to support it.
essential_questions: How can I clearly express my opinion?
What reasons can I provide to support my opinion?
Why is it important to back up my opinions with evidence?
learning_targets: I can express my opinion on a familiar topic.
I can support my opinion with reasons and examples.
I can write a series of sentences to explain my thoughts.
sample_assessment: What is your opinion about school uniforms? Choose the best supporting detail to go with your opinion.

benchmark: Compare and contrast information, concepts, and ideas.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to compare and contrast information, concepts, and ideas. Items may include prompts that ask students to write a comparison essay or create a Venn diagram, highlighting similarities and differences between two subjects.
essential_questions: What are the similarities and differences between these two topics?
How can I organize my comparison effectively?
Why is it useful to compare and contrast information?
learning_targets: I can compare two concepts and identify their similarities.
I can contrast two ideas and highlight their differences.
I can organize my thoughts clearly when comparing and contrasting.
sample_assessment: Which of the following best compares and contrasts cats and dogs?

benchmark: Develop questions to obtain and clarify information.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to formulate questions that clarify information on a given topic. Items may include scenarios where students must choose the most effective question to ask or create their own questions based on provided information.
essential_questions: How can asking the right questions lead to better understanding?
What types of questions are most effective in different situations?
learning_targets: I can develop questions to clarify information.
I can identify the purpose of different types of questions.
sample_assessment: Which question would best help you understand the topic of climate change? A) What is climate change? B) Why is climate change important? C) How does climate change affect the environment?

benchmark: Conduct research and write a detailed plan (e.g., a trip to a country where the target language is spoken).
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to create a detailed plan for a research project, including objectives, resources, and steps for execution. Items may involve evaluating different research methods or planning a trip with specific cultural elements.
essential_questions: What steps are necessary to conduct effective research?
How can planning enhance the success of a project?
learning_targets: I can conduct research to gather information.
I can create a detailed plan for a project.
sample_assessment: What is the first step in planning a trip to a Spanish-speaking country? A) Book flights B) Research cultural practices C) Pack your bags

benchmark: Develop a draft of a plan that addresses purpose, audience, logical sequence, and a time frame for completion.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on the components of a well-structured draft, requiring students to address purpose, audience, logical sequence, and time frame. Items may include evaluating drafts or creating outlines based on given criteria.
essential_questions: How does understanding your audience influence your writing?
What elements are essential for a logical sequence in a plan?
learning_targets: I can develop a draft that includes purpose and audience.
I can organize my ideas logically in a plan.
sample_assessment: Which element is most important to consider when drafting a plan for a presentation? A) The color of the slides B) The audience's interests C) The length of the presentation

benchmark: Recognize similarities and differences in practices and perspectives used across cultures (e.g., holidays, family life) to understand one's own and others' ways of thinking.
item_specs: Assessment items should encourage students to identify and compare cultural practices and perspectives. Items may include scenarios where students analyze similarities and differences in cultural traditions or practices.
essential_questions: How do cultural practices shape our perspectives?
What can we learn from comparing different cultures?
learning_targets: I can recognize similarities and differences in cultural practices.
I can reflect on how my culture influences my perspective.
sample_assessment: Which of the following is a similarity between American and Mexican holiday celebrations? A) Both celebrate Christmas B) Both celebrate Thanksgiving C) Both have unique traditional foods

benchmark: Demonstrate awareness and appreciation of cultural practices and expressions in daily activities.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on demonstrating awareness of cultural practices in daily life. Items may involve identifying cultural expressions in various contexts or reflecting on how these practices influence daily activities.
essential_questions: Why is it important to appreciate cultural expressions?
How do cultural practices influence our daily lives?
learning_targets: I can demonstrate awareness of cultural practices.
I can appreciate cultural expressions in my daily activities.
sample_assessment: Which of the following is an example of a cultural expression in daily life? A) Eating pizza B) Celebrating a traditional festival C) Watching a movie

benchmark: Examine significant historic events and their impact on the target culture(s).
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to analyze and evaluate the significance of specific historic events and their effects on the target culture. Items may include scenarios, primary source documents, or multimedia presentations that prompt critical thinking and connections to contemporary issues.
essential_questions: What are some key historic events that shaped the target culture?
How do these events influence the culture today?
In what ways can we draw parallels between historic events in the target culture and our own?
learning_targets: I can identify significant historic events in the target culture.
I can explain the impact of these events on the culture.
I can compare historic events from the target culture to those in my own culture.
sample_assessment: Which historic event had the most significant impact on the development of the target culture?

benchmark: Identify cultural artifacts, symbols, and images of the target culture(s) and compare them to one's own.
item_specs: Items should focus on identifying and comparing cultural artifacts, symbols, and images from the target culture and the students' own culture. Students may be asked to analyze visual representations and discuss their meanings and significance.
essential_questions: What cultural artifacts represent the target culture?
How do these artifacts compare to those in my own culture?
What do symbols tell us about a culture's values and beliefs?
learning_targets: I can identify cultural artifacts from the target culture.
I can compare symbols from the target culture to those from my own.
I can explain the significance of cultural images.
sample_assessment: Which of the following artifacts is a symbol of the target culture's values?

benchmark: Access information in the target language to reinforce previously acquired knowledge.
item_specs: Assessment items should involve accessing information in the target language that reinforces previously acquired knowledge. This may include reading comprehension exercises, listening activities, or discussions that require students to synthesize and apply what they have learned.
essential_questions: How can I use the target language to reinforce what I already know?
What types of resources are available in the target language?
How does accessing information in the target language enhance my understanding?
learning_targets: I can find information in the target language to support my learning.
I can summarize information I have learned in the target language.
I can discuss previously acquired knowledge using the target language.
sample_assessment: What is one way to access information in the target language that reinforces your learning?

benchmark: Access new information on topics of personal interest using the target language and authentic sources.
item_specs: Items should encourage students to seek out and evaluate new information on topics of personal interest using the target language and authentic sources. This may include research projects, presentations, or discussions that require critical thinking and application of language skills.
essential_questions: What topics am I interested in that I can explore in the target language?
How do authentic sources enhance my understanding of these topics?
What strategies can I use to find reliable information in the target language?
learning_targets: I can identify topics of personal interest to explore in the target language.
I can use authentic sources to gather new information.
I can present my findings in the target language.
sample_assessment: Which of the following is an authentic source for researching a topic of personal interest in the target language?

benchmark: Recognize language patterns and cultural differences when comparing own language and culture to the target language and culture.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on recognizing and analyzing language patterns and cultural differences between the student's own language and the target language. This may involve comparative essays, discussions, or activities that highlight linguistic and cultural nuances.
essential_questions: What are some key differences between my language and the target language?
How do these differences reflect cultural values?
In what ways can understanding language patterns enhance my communication?
learning_targets: I can identify language patterns in the target language.
I can compare cultural differences between my culture and the target culture.
I can explain how language reflects cultural values.
sample_assessment: Which of the following statements best describes a cultural difference between my culture and the target culture?

benchmark: Give examples of cognates, borrowed words, and idiomatic expressions to compare the target language to own.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to identify and provide examples of cognates, borrowed words, and idiomatic expressions in both the target language and their own language. Items may include matching, multiple-choice, or short-answer formats that encourage critical thinking about language similarities and differences.
essential_questions: What are cognates and how do they help in learning a new language?
How do borrowed words reflect cultural exchange?
What role do idiomatic expressions play in understanding a language?
learning_targets: I can identify cognates between the target language and my own.
I can give examples of borrowed words from the target language.
I can explain the meaning of idiomatic expressions in context.
sample_assessment: Which of the following is a cognate of the English word 'information' in Spanish?

benchmark: Compare and contrast cultural traditions, celebrations, and daily routines of the target culture and own.
item_specs: Assessment items should encourage students to analyze and compare cultural traditions, celebrations, and daily routines between the target culture and their own. Items may include written responses, graphic organizers, or presentations that require synthesis of information and critical analysis.
essential_questions: How do cultural traditions shape the identity of a community?
In what ways do celebrations reflect the values of a culture?
How do daily routines differ between cultures and what do they signify?
learning_targets: I can compare cultural traditions from the target culture and my own.
I can describe a celebration from the target culture.
I can analyze the significance of daily routines in different cultures.
sample_assessment: Which of the following best describes a common celebration in the target culture?

benchmark: Use the target language to participate in activities for personal enjoyment and enrichment.
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on students' ability to use the target language in various activities that promote personal enjoyment and enrichment. Items may include scenarios where students must choose appropriate language responses or describe activities they enjoy in the target language.
essential_questions: How can using the target language enhance my personal interests?
What activities do I enjoy that can be done in the target language?
How does language use influence my engagement in cultural activities?
learning_targets: I can describe activities I enjoy in the target language.
I can participate in a conversation about personal interests using the target language.
I can identify resources in the target language that enhance my hobbies.
sample_assessment: Which of the following activities can you describe in the target language to express your interests?

benchmark: Interact with people of other cultures using the target language in a variety of settings.
item_specs: Assessment items should assess students' ability to interact with speakers of other cultures using the target language in various contexts. Items may include role-playing scenarios, dialogues, or written interactions that require appropriate language use and cultural awareness.
essential_questions: How can I effectively communicate with people from different cultures?
What strategies can I use to enhance my interactions in the target language?
How does understanding cultural context improve my language skills?
learning_targets: I can engage in conversations with speakers of the target language.
I can use the target language in different social settings.
I can demonstrate cultural awareness during interactions.
sample_assessment: In which scenario would you use the target language to interact with someone from another culture?

benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of familiar topics and frequently used expressions supported by a variety of actions.
item_specs: Assessment items should include scenarios where students must demonstrate understanding through matching actions to expressions or responding appropriately to prompts. Items may involve audio clips or visual cues that require students to select the correct response based on familiar topics.
essential_questions: How do I express my feelings in different situations?
What actions can help me communicate effectively?
How can I use familiar expressions to interact with others?
learning_targets: I can recognize and respond to familiar expressions.
I can demonstrate understanding through actions.
I can engage in simple conversations using learned phrases.
sample_assessment: Which action best matches the greeting 'Hola'? A) Wave, B) Frown, C) Yawn, D) Sit down.

benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of short conversations, in familiar contexts. Greeting people and saying goodbye (S-1.M) How are you doing? (S-1.N) Introductions (S-1.O) Listen to questions about student life and choose the appropriate response (S-4.N) Listen to conversations about making plans (S-7.R)
item_specs: Assessment items should present short audio clips of conversations in familiar contexts. Students will listen and select appropriate responses or identify key phrases. Items may also include multiple-choice questions based on scenarios involving greetings and farewells.
essential_questions: How do I start and end a conversation?
What are common phrases used in greetings?
How can I respond appropriately in a conversation?
learning_targets: I can understand and respond to greetings.
I can listen to conversations and identify key points.
I can engage in simple dialogues about my life.
sample_assessment: What is the appropriate response to '¿Cómo estás?' A) Estoy bien, B) Adiós, C) Gracias, D) Sí.

benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of short, simple messages and announcements on familiar topics. The Spanish alphabet (S-1.A) Listen and fill in a form with personal information: letters and numbers (S-1.P) Listen and choose the appropriate response to basic questions (S-1.Q) Listen and identify the person from the description (S-2.O) Listen and recognize classes and school supplies (S-3.Q) Listen to questions about student life and choose the appropriate response (S-4.N) Listen and match the statement to the image (S-5.R) Listen to statements about actions and habits related to food (S-6.R) Listen to conversations about making plans (S-7.R)
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on listening comprehension, where students listen to short messages or announcements and answer questions based on the content. Items may include fill-in-the-blank formats or multiple-choice questions that assess understanding of familiar topics.
essential_questions: How can I understand announcements in my school?
What types of messages do I encounter daily?
How do I extract key information from short messages?
learning_targets: I can listen to and understand simple messages.
I can fill in forms with personal information.
I can identify key points from announcements.
sample_assessment: What is the main idea of the announcement: 'La clase de español comienza a las diez'? A) Spanish class starts at ten, B) Spanish class is canceled, C) Spanish class is at lunch, D) Spanish class is in the library.

benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of key points on familiar topics presented through a variety of media.
item_specs: Assessment items should require students to analyze various media presentations, such as audio, video, or written formats, to identify key points. Items may involve summarizing information or answering questions that demonstrate comprehension of familiar topics.
essential_questions: How can I identify important information from different media?
What strategies help me understand key points in presentations?
How does the medium affect my understanding of a topic?
learning_targets: I can identify key points from audio and visual media.
I can summarize information presented in various formats.
I can discuss familiar topics based on media content.
sample_assessment: After watching a video about school supplies, what is one key point mentioned? A) School starts at 8 AM, B) You need a backpack, C) Lunch is served, D) There are no classes.

benchmark: Demonstrate understanding of simple stories or narratives. Listen to people describe themselves and others (S-2.P) Listen to people talk about the school day (S-3.R) Listen to people talk about their school and everything in it (S-4.O) Listen to people talk about their families (S-5.S) Listen to people talk about their favorite foods and drinks (S-6.S) Listen to people talk about sports, hobbies, and free time (S-7.S)
item_specs: Assessment items should focus on listening to simple narratives or stories and require students to answer questions that demonstrate their understanding. Items may include sequencing events, identifying characters, or summarizing the main idea of the story.
essential_questions: How do I understand the main idea of a story?
What details are important in a narrative?
How can I retell a story I have heard?
learning_targets: I can listen to and understand simple stories.
I can identify characters and events in a narrative.
I can retell a story using key details.
sample_assessment: What is the main character's favorite food in the story? A) Pizza, B) Salad, C) Ice cream, D) Tacos.

benchmark: Follow directions or instructions to complete a task when expressed in short conversations.
item_specs: Assessment items should present short conversational scenarios where students must interpret and follow verbal instructions. Items may include multiple-choice questions that ask students to select the correct action based on the given directions.
essential_questions: How do I interpret spoken instructions?
What strategies can I use to follow directions accurately?
learning_targets: I can listen to short conversations and understand the instructions.
I can follow directions to complete a task.
sample_assessment: If the teacher says, 'Draw a circle and color it blue,' what should you do?
